hypothesis: Introducing a differentiable continuous thickness space coupled with a physics-informed, tolerance-aware loss that accounts for fabrication-induced thickness variations (via a stochastic perturbation model within the transfer-matrix verifier) will enable CHROMA to generalize to unseen target spectra with lower average color error (∆E) under realistic manufacturing variability, achieving a mean ∆E < 15 on held-out structures at 0°, 30°, and 60° incidence.
